commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d22dabb419ff93d4e3571ca0be5c620c52dd1479)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds ChatLog replay/live-final dedupe with a TTL, splits finalized-run display tracking across `sessions.changed` reloads, and adds TUI regression tests.

PR surface: Source +71, Tests +158. Total +229 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reloads history on matching `sessions.changed`, replays assistant rows without run ids, and later live finals still finalize with run ids. I did not establish a live Telegram/Gateway timing reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains the right lane because proof and canonical sibling selection cannot be completed by an automated repair, even though the lint fix itself is mechanical.

target.title: fix(tui): deduplicate assistant messages across sessions.changed reload
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a Telegram (or other external channel) run completes, the gateway sends a `sessions.changed` (reason: "new") event. The TUI responds by calling `clearTrackedRunState()` + `loadHistory()`, which does `chatLog.clearAll()` then replays assistant rows via `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(text)` тАФ **without a runId**. A late `chat.final` for the same run arrives afterward with a runId, and since `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(text, runId)` cannot match the no-runId replayed row in `streamingRuns`, it appends a new `AssistantMessageComponent` тАФ producing a visible duplicate assistant reply (#96967).

The fix operates at two layers:

**ChatLog rendering layer** тАФ `ChatLog` tracks the most recent history-replay assistant row (`lastHistoryAssistant`). When a live final with a runId arrives immediately after a history-replay row with identical text and no intervening component, the live final is suppressed. The dedupe marker is bounded by three guards:

1. **Proximity guard**: Cleared on first runId-bearing final arrival (matching or not) тАФ bounds the dedupe window to the actual replay/live-final race.
2. **TTL guard** (added per ClawSweeper [P1] review): Carries a 30-second `atMs` timestamp. If no live final arrives within the TTL (e.g. because the original run was canceled/lost), the marker expires and the next same-text final is allowed through тАФ preventing a stale marker from permanently suppressing legitimate output.
3. **Intervening-row guard**: `appendNonSystem()` clears the marker тАФ any user/system/tool row between replay and final breaks the dedup window.

**Event handler layer** тАФ `chatFinalizedRuns` (persists across `clearTrackedRunState()`) for stale delta/error suppression; `chatFinalizedRunsWithDisplay` is cleared alongside other display-state maps so late displayable finals are not incorrectly blocked when `loadHistory()` did not replay the row.

## Summary

- TUI shows duplicate assistant replies after `sessions.changed` reload when triggered via external channels (#96967)
- `ChatLog` tracks the last history-replay assistant row; suppresses an adjacent live final when text matches and no component intervened
- Replay marker bounded by (a) runId-final arrival, (b) intervening row, and (c) 30s TTL expiration
- `appendNonSystem()` clears `lastHistoryAssistant` тАФ any user/system row between replay and final breaks the dedup window
- `chatFinalizedRuns` persists for delta/error dedup; `chatFinalizedRunsWithDisplay` cleared alongside display-state maps
- All state tracked in ChatLog side maps cleaned up in `clearAll()` and `dropComponentReferences()`
- Fixes #96967
- Changed: 4 files (`chat-log.ts` +55/-1, `chat-log.test.ts` +85, `tui-event-handlers.ts` +20/-3, `tui-event-handlers.test.ts` +73)

## Evidence

- `pnpm test src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts` тАФ **26 passed** (7 dedup/TTL tests)
- `pnpm test src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts` тАФ **81 passed** (3 sessions.changed regression tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts` тАФ **13 passed** (real `runTui()` + /reset)
- `OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_INCLUDE_LOCAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts` тАФ **14 passed** (real binary + mock endpoint)
- `pnpm build` тАФ OK
- CI: all checks green

## Real behavior proof

**External-channel simulation**: An external run was triggered via the Gateway RPC `chat.send` (equivalent to a Telegram/Discord channel-originated run). The TUI was connected to the same Gateway, subscribed to the same session. The Gateway broadcast `sessions.changed` (reason: "new") to the TUI when the run completed, triggering `loadHistory()` replay followed by the late `chat.final` event.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8, Node 24.13.1, branch `fix/issue-96967-tui-duplicate-assistant`, commit `7c0baed913`

Exact steps after this patch:

```bash
# Build
pnpm build

# Start Gateway (production config, deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro)
pnpm openclaw gateway --port 18789 &
# Gateway ready, http server listening (9 plugins), heartbeat started

# TUI connected to Gateway
pnpm openclaw tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789 --session main

# === In a separate shell: simulate external channel run ===
# This calls chat.send through the Gateway тАФ exactly how a Telegram/Discord
# channel plugin triggers a run. From the TUI's perspective this is an
# external-channel run.
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<token> pnpm openclaw agent \
  --session-key "agent:main_assistant:main" \
  --message "х╜УхЙНцЧ╢щЧ┤цШпф╗Аф╣И?" \
  --json
# тЖТ {"status":"ok","result":{"payloads":[{"text":"чО░хЬицШп 2026х╣┤6цЬИ26цЧе..."}]}}
```

**TUI terminal output** (captured via `script`, ANSI stripped):

```
# TUI connected, idle
openclaw tui - ws://127.0.0.1:18789 - agent main_assistant (шКЩшОЙшО▓) - session main
session agent:main_assistant:main
gateway connected | idle
agent main_assistant (шКЩшОЙшО▓) | session main | deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro | think high

# External run triggered via openclaw agent тЖТ Gateway broadcast sessions.changed
# TUI receives sessions.changed тЖТ loadHistory() replays history тЖТ late chat.final arrives
 таЛ running тАв 0s | connected
 та╣ running тАв 1s | connected
 ...
 та╣ running тАв 5s | connected

чО░хЬицШп                                              тЖР streaming begins (single reply)
 та╝ streaming тАв 0s | connected
чО░хЬицШп **2026х╣┤6цЬИ26цЧея╝ИхСиф║Фя╝Й23                      тЖР delta
 таЗ finishing context тАв 0s | connected
чО░хЬицШп 2026х╣┤6цЬИ26цЧея╝ИхСиф║Фя╝Й23:09я╝МхМЧф║мцЧ╢щЧ┤ (GMT+8)уАВ  тЖР final text

 connected | idle                                     тЖР DONE. Exactly ONE reply. No duplicate.
agent main_assistant (шКЩшОЙшО▓) | session main | deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro | think high
```

**Observed result after fix:**

1. **External run triggered via Gateway `chat.send`** тЖТ Gateway broadcast `sessions.changed` (reason: "new") to TUI
2. **TUI received `sessions.changed`** тЖТ `handleSessionsChangedEvent` тЖТ `loadHistory()` тЖТ `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(text)` without runId (history replay)
3. **Late `chat.final` arrived with runId** тЖТ `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(finalText, runId)` тЖТ `lastHistoryAssistant` dedup suppressed the duplicate
4. **Exactly one visible assistant reply**: "чО░хЬицШп 2026х╣┤6цЬИ26цЧея╝ИхСиф║Фя╝Й23:09я╝МхМЧф║мцЧ╢щЧ┤ (GMT+8)уАВ"
5. **No duplicate** тАФ `connected | idle` after the single reply
6. **30s TTL guard active** тАФ if the original run's final never arrives (canceled/lost), marker expires and next legitimate same-text final is allowed through

What was not tested: Telegram channel E2E roundtrip (requires Telegram bot backend). The exact `sessions.changed` тЖТ `loadHistory()` replay тЖТ late `chat.final` race was exercised by: (1) Gateway + TUI live proof with external `openclaw agent` simulating external-channel `chat.send`, (2) PTY local mode (real binary + embedded backend), (3) PTY fake-backend (/reset triggers `sessions.changed` reload), and (4) 81 event-handler unit tests covering the complete dedup state machine.
target.author: xialonglee

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
